title: NSIC Young Professional Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 17 Posts
organization: National Small Industries Corporation Limited (NSIC), A Government of India Enterprise under Ministry of MSME, New Delhi
department: Human Resources, NSIC Corporate Office / Subsidiaries
post_name: Young Professional (on Contract Basis) — 12 Domain Specialisations
short_info: NSIC is recruiting 17 Young Professional posts on contract basis across 12 domains with relevant graduation/PG/professional qualification, last date 08 June 2026.

important_dates.eligibility / qualification cut-off date: 08-06-2026 (qualifications and experience must be acquired on or before this date)
important_dates.interview_date: To be notified to shortlisted candidates by email
important_dates.joining date (after offer): Within 15 days of offer of engagement

application_fee.details: The official notification does not mention any application fee for NSIC Young Professional Recruitment 2026. Candidates should verify on the NSIC Career portal (www.nsic.co.in) at the time of applying, as any applicable fee details will be specified on the online application portal.

age_limit.details: Upper age limit is 40 years as on the closing date of advertisement (08-06-2026). No minimum age is specified beyond the implied requirement of 2 years of relevant post-qualification work experience. Age relaxation for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates is as per Government of India guidelines, where applicable.

qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions (qualification and experience) as on 08-06-2026. Post-wise qualification and experience requirements are detailed below. Only candidates with the relevant domain qualification and minimum 2 years of work experience in the relevant field are eligible to apply.

vacancy_details.Post Name: Young Professional – Digital Media & Social Media Management
Young Professional – Digital Marketing, E-Commerce etc.
Young Professional – Market Research, Data Interpretation & Financial Analysis
Young Professional – Venture Equity Fund Senior Analyst
Young Professional – IT Specialist for Venture Capital Fund

vacancy_details.Condition: Head Office, New Delhi (may be posted anywhere in India at NSIC's discretion)
12 days per year on pro rata basis
Initially 1 year; extendable by 1 year at a time up to maximum 5 years (subject to KPI-based annual performance review)
Not exceeding 5% of monthly remuneration subject to satisfactory performance and maximum cap of Rs. 70,000/-
30 days notice (by either party) or compensation in lieu thereof
Within 15 days of offer of engagement; failing which offer stands withdrawn automatically

salary_details.pay_scale: Rs. 40,000/- to Rs. 70,000/- per month (all inclusive). Annual increment not exceeding 5% subject to satisfactory performance and maximum cap of Rs. 70,000/-. No DA, HRA, CGHS, or other allowances.

selection_process: Selection is through shortlisting of applications followed by an interaction / interview by the NSIC Selection Committee. Mere submission of application or fulfillment of eligibility criteria does not entitle a candidate to be called for interview. Call letters and other information will be sent to shortlisted candidates by email only and may also be published on the NSIC corporate website. NSIC reserves the right to upgrade eligibility criteria if a large number of applications are received.
Applications received within the deadline will be screened for eligibility as per the advertisement. Candidates who meet the prescribed qualification

and age criteria will be shortlisted based on the relevance and quality of their profile. Incomplete applications will be summarily rejected. Any request to add additional information after submission will not be entertained.
Shortlisted candidates will be called for an interaction / interview before the NSIC Selection Committee. Date

and venue of the interaction will be communicated by email only to the registered email ID. Candidates must keep their email ID active for at least 3 months from the date of application. NSIC will not be responsible for non-delivery or late delivery of email due to any reason.

Selected candidates must submit a Statement of Good Health from a recognized Physician prior to commencement of work. Antecedent/police verification of selected candidates will be done as per latest instructions issued by the Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA). If police verification is received as negative
the contract will cease to exist with immediate effect without any notice.
how_to_apply: Candidates shall apply online through the CAREER section of www.nsic.co.in only. No other mode of application will be accepted. The online application link is active from 23-05-2026. The application process is in 3 stages. Candidates should keep all documents ready before starting the application as incomplete applications cannot be modified after submission.
Stage 1 — Registration: Fill in Name, Mobile Number, Email ID, and Date of Birth. Verify email via the verification link sent to the registered email ID. Login and fill in basic details.
Stage 2 — Document Upload (Self-attested copies):
Aadhaar Card
PAN Card
Caste / PwBD Certificate (if applicable)
Passport-size colour photograph with white background (JPG/JPEG/PNG, recent, under 100 KB)
Signature (JPG/JPEG/PNG, under 100 KB)
Class X Certificate / School Leaving Certificate — as Date of Birth proof
Mark-sheets of each year / consolidated mark-sheet of Graduation / Post Graduation
Graduation / Post Graduation Degree Certificate
Experience Certificates (Service Certificate showing start and end of employment OR Appointment Letter + Resignation/Relieving Letter OR salary slips duly stamped and signed)
Salary slips / salary certificates as proof of current compensation
Detailed CV
CGPA to percentage conversion proof from University/Institute (if applicable)
Gazette notification / affidavit for name/surname change (if applicable)
Company name change proof / inter-group transfer proof (if applicable)

Stage 3 — Submission: After completing all stages, an Application Number will be generated and sent to the registered mobile number via SMS and email. This Application Number must be preserved for all future references.
Documents to be Produced at Interview / Interaction:
Printout of submitted online application
All original documents (listed above) along with self-attested photocopies
Class X Certificate / School Leaving Certificate (original) for Date of Birth
All original mark-sheets and degree certificates (Graduation / Post Graduation)
Original experience / service certificates and salary slips
Original Aadhaar Card, PAN Card
Original caste certificate / PwBD certificate (if applicable)
Statement of Good Health from a recognized Physician (for joining, if selected)
Apply Online at: www.nsic.co.in → Career Section → NSIC/HR/YP/01/2026 → Apply Online
For queries: Visit www.nsic.co.in | All corrigenda/updates regarding engagement process will be notified on NSIC's website only.
important_instructions: THIS IS NOT A REGULAR EMPLOYMENT. Engagement of Young Professionals is purely on a contractual basis for consultancy purposes. Young Professionals are not entitled to any benefit or compensation available to regular NSIC employees, including DA, HRA, CGHS, Medical reimbursement, or Provident Fund. The engagement is on a full-time basis. The selected candidate cannot take up any other assignment in any other company during the period of engagement. Candidates may apply for only one post. If more than one application is submitted, only the first application (by system-generated date and time) will be considered and the remaining will be summarily rejected. Prescribed qualifications and experience must have been acquired on or before 08-06-2026. Qualifications or experience acquired after the cut-off will not be considered. Incomplete applications will be summarily rejected. Once submitted, no request to add or modify information will be entertained under any circumstances. NSIC reserves the right to upgrade eligibility criteria and increase or decrease the number of vacancies in any category without assigning any reason. Call letters and all future communications will be sent by email only. Candidates must ensure their registered email ID is active for at least 3 months. NSIC bears no responsibility for non-delivery of emails. Shortlisting for interview is at NSIC's sole discretion — fulfilling eligibility criteria does not guarantee a call for interview. The selected candidate must join within 15 days of the offer of engagement. Failure to join within this period will result in automatic withdrawal of the offer. Income Tax and other applicable taxes will be deducted at source. NSIC will issue TDS certificates. GST, as applicable, shall be admissible to Young Professionals. The Young Professional is responsible for paying their own Income Tax as applicable. NSIC or the Competent Authority may terminate the contract at any time by giving 30 days' notice or paying 30 days' compensation in lieu thereof, without assigning any reason. Antecedent/police verification of selected candidates will be conducted as per MHA guidelines. A negative police verification report will result in immediate termination of the contract without any notice. No abbreviations should be used in the application where full forms are indicated — applications with abbreviations in such fields are liable for rejection. NSIC reserves the right to cancel, modify, withdraw, or postpone this recruitment notice at any time without assigning any reason. All corrigenda and updates will be published on www.nsic.co.in only — candidates should check the website regularly.

faq.question: Q1. What is NSIC Young Professional Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The NSIC Young Professional Recruitment 2026 is a contractual hiring initiative by the National Small Industries Corporation Limited (NSIC) under the Ministry of MSME, Government of India. It aims to engage qualified professionals across multiple domains including IT, Finance, Marketing, Engineering, Legal, and Digital Media. The recruitment is purely on a contract basis and does not provide permanent government employment benefits.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available in NSIC Young Professional Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 17 Young Professional vacancies have been announced under NSIC Recruitment 2026. These posts are distributed across 12 specialised domains, including Software Development, Digital Marketing, Company Secretary, Civil Engineering, Electrical Engineering, and MSME Business Outreach. The number of vacancies may be revised depending on organizational requirements.

faq.question: Q3. What is the eligibility criteria for NSIC Young Professional posts?
faq.answer: Candidates must hold a relevant graduation, post-graduation, or professional qualification depending on the post applied for. In addition, a minimum of 2 years of work experience in the relevant field is mandatory. Applicants must also ensure that their qualifications and experience are acquired on or before the cut-off date mentioned in the notification.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for NSIC Young Professional Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The upper age limit for NSIC Young Professional posts is 40 years as on 08 June 2026. Age relaxation is applicable for reserved categories such as SC, ST, OBC-NCL, and PwBD candidates as per Government of India rules. However, candidates must still meet the minimum experience requirement along with age eligibility.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary offered in NSIC Young Professional Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selected candidates will receive a consolidated monthly pay ranging from Rs. 40,000 to Rs. 70,000, depending on the post and experience. There are no additional allowances such as DA, HRA, or medical benefits. However, performance-based annual increments of up to 5% may be granted, subject to a maximum ceiling.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for NSIC Young Professional Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process consists of shortlisting of applications followed by an interview or interaction. Candidates are shortlisted based on their qualifications, experience, and domain relevance. Final selection is made through interview performance, and only shortlisted candidates are called via email for further stages.

faq.question: Q7. Is NSIC Young Professional Recruitment 2026 a permanent job?
faq.answer: No, this recruitment is strictly contractual in nature. Selected candidates will be engaged initially for one year, extendable up to a maximum of five years based on performance. It does not offer permanent government job status, pension, or regular employee benefits.

faq.question: Q8. What is the application process for NSIC Young Professional Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must apply online through the official NSIC website (www.nsic.co.in) under the Careers section. The process includes registration, filling the application form, uploading documents, and submitting the form online. After submission, candidates receive a system-generated application number for future reference.

faq.question: Q9. What is the last date to apply for NSIC Young Professional Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application for NSIC Young Professional Recruitment 2026 is 08 June 2026. Candidates are advised to complete the application process well before the deadline to avoid technical issues. No applications will be accepted after the closing date.

faq.question: Q10. Where can I download the NSIC Young Professional Recruitment 2026 notification PDF?
faq.answer: The official notification PDF for NSIC Young Professional Recruitment 2026 can be downloaded from the NSIC official website (www.nsic.co.in). It contains complete details regarding vacancies, eligibility, salary, selection process, and application guidelines. Candidates should always refer to the official advertisement before applying online.
